name: Security: OWASP Top 10 & Web App Security
description: Prevent common web vulnerabilities by implementing OWASP Top 10 mitigation strategies.
template:
OWASP Top 10 Prevention:

1. **A01: Broken Access Control**
   - Use CODEOWNERS for code review enforcement
   - Implement role-based access control (RBAC)
   - Validate permissions on every API endpoint
   - Never trust client-side auth; verify server-side
   - Pattern: if (!user.hasRole('admin')) return 401;

2. **A02: Cryptographic Failures**
   - Use HTTPS everywhere (enforce with HSTS header)
   - Never log passwords or tokens
   - Use bcrypt/argon2 for password hashing (not SHA256)
   - Rotate secrets regularly; use secret managers
   - Pattern: await bcrypt.compare(password, hash)

3. **A03: Injection**
   - Prevent SQL Injection: Use parameterized queries
   - Prevent Command Injection: Never exec() user input
   - Prevent XSS: Escape HTML output, use DOMPurify for rich text
   - Validate ALL user input: whitelist, not blacklist
   - Pattern: sql.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?', [userId])

4. **A04: Insecure Design**
   - Design threat models before implementation
   - Document security requirements in PRs
   - Use security checklists in code review
   - Threat modeling: STRIDE, attack trees

5. **A05: Security Misconfiguration**
   - Disable debug mode in production
   - Remove unnecessary HTTP methods (HEAD, TRACE)
   - Set security headers: CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options
   - Use dependabot/snyk for dependency scanning

6. **A06: Vulnerable & Outdated Components**
   - Run npm audit weekly; fix HIGH/CRITICAL issues
   - Use Snyk or Dependabot in CI/CD
   - Maintain dependency update schedule
   - Monitor CVE databases for known vulnerabilities

7. **A07: Authentication Failures**
   - Implement multi-factor authentication (MFA)
   - Use secure password policies: min 12 chars, complexity
   - Implement rate limiting: max 5 failed logins
   - Use JWT with short expiry (15 min) + refresh tokens

8. **A08: Software & Data Integrity Failures**
   - Sign release artifacts
   - Verify third-party package integrity
   - Use SRI (Subresource Integrity) for CDN scripts
   - Implement CI/CD pipeline signing

9. **A09: Logging & Monitoring Failures**
   - Log all security-relevant events (login, auth failures, data access)
   - Monitor for suspicious patterns
   - Set up alerts for anomalies
   - Retain logs for 90+ days

10. **A10: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)**
    - Validate and whitelist URLs before requests
    - Disable redirects to internal IPs
    - Use allowlists for outbound requests
    - Monitor outbound traffic for anomalies

name: Security: Content Security Policy & Security Headers
description: Implement comprehensive security headers to protect against XSS, clickjacking, and injection attacks.
template:
Security Headers Best Practices:

1. **Content Security Policy (CSP)**
   - Use nonce-based CSP (not 'unsafe-inline')
   - Directive: default-src 'self'
   - Directive: script-src 'self' 'nonce-{RANDOM}' https://trusted.com
   - Directive: style-src 'self' 'nonce-{RANDOM}' https://fonts.googleapis.com
   - Prevent inline script execution entirely
   - Pattern: Generate unique nonce per request in middleware

2. **HTTP Security Headers**
   - Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload
   - X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff (prevent MIME sniffing)
   - X-Frame-Options: DENY (prevent clickjacking)
   - X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block (legacy; CSP is preferred)
   - Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin

3. **Permissions Policy (Feature Policy)**
   - Directive: camera=(), microphone=() (disable unused features)
   - Directive: geolocation=() (restrict location access)
   - Directive: payment=() (restrict payment APIs)
   - Prevents abuse of browser APIs

4. **Cookie Security**
   - Set HttpOnly flag (prevents JS access)
   - Set Secure flag (HTTPS only)
   - Set SameSite=Strict (prevents CSRF)
   - Pattern: Set-Cookie: sessionId=abc; HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Strict

5. **Implementation in Next.js**
   - Use middleware.ts to set headers
   - Pattern: response.headers.set('X-Frame-Options', 'DENY')
   - Generate nonce per request in layout/middleware
   - Test CSP in Report-Only mode first
source: GitHub: OWASP CSP Cheat Sheet, MDN security headers

name: Security: Input Validation & Sanitization
description: Prevent injection attacks and malformed data through comprehensive input validation.
template:
Input Validation Best Practices:

1. **Server-Side Validation (Mandatory)**
   - Never trust client-side validation alone
   - Use Zod, Yup, or io-ts for schema validation
   - Validate shape, type, length, format
   - Reject malformed requests early
   - Pattern: const schema = z.object({ email: z.string().email(), age: z.number().min(0).max(120) })

2. **Request Body Validation**
   - Define strict request schemas
   - Reject additional unexpected fields (strict mode)
   - Validate file uploads: type, size, virus scan
   - Pattern: express.json({ limit: '10mb' }); // with request size limit

3. **URL & Query Parameter Validation**
   - Whitelist allowed parameters
   - Validate format: UUID, slug, numeric IDs
   - Prevent directory traversal: validate path segments
   - Pattern: const id = z.string().uuid().parse(req.params.id)

4. **HTML & Rich Text Sanitization**
   - Use DOMPurify for user-generated HTML
   - Never use dangerouslySetInnerHTML without sanitization
   - Whitelist allowed tags and attributes
   - Pattern: import DOMPurify from 'isomorphic-dompurify'; const clean = DOMPurify.sanitize(userHTML)

5. **Error Messages**
   - Never reveal implementation details in errors
   - Log detailed errors server-side
   - Return generic messages to clients
   - Pattern: throw new ApiError('Invalid input', 400); // don't expose DB schema

6. **File Upload Security**
   - Validate MIME type (check magic bytes, not extension)
   - Scan uploads with ClamAV or VirusTotal
   - Store in secure location outside web root
   - Generate random filename; discard user filename
   - Pattern: crypto.randomBytes(16).toString('hex') + '.jpg'

name: Security: Vulnerability Scanning & Dependency Management
description: Establish automated vulnerability scanning and dependency management to prevent supply chain attacks.
template:
Vulnerability Management:

1. **Dependency Auditing**
   - Run npm audit in CI/CD pipeline
   - Integrate Snyk, Dependabot, or WhiteSource
   - Fail build on HIGH/CRITICAL vulnerabilities
   - Review and merge security updates weekly
   - Pattern: npm audit --audit-level=high

2. **Automated Updates**
   - Enable Dependabot/GitHub security updates
   - Auto-approve patch updates (v1.2.3 → v1.2.4)
   - Require review for minor/major updates
   - Set up automation schedule: Monday 9am

3. **Software Composition Analysis (SCA)**
   - Scan dependencies for known CVEs
   - Check licenses (GPL, AGPL conflicts)
   - Identify outdated packages (lagging >6 months)
   - Monitor supply chain risks (typosquatting, abandoned packages)

4. **Static Application Security Testing (SAST)**
   - Use ESLint security plugins: eslint-plugin-security
   - SonarQube or similar for code quality + security
   - Scan for hardcoded secrets (git-secrets, TruffleHog)
   - Pattern: npm install -D eslint-plugin-security

5. **Secret Rotation**
   - Rotate database passwords quarterly
   - Rotate API keys when employees leave
   - Revoke compromised tokens immediately
   - Use secret manager: AWS Secrets Manager, Vault, 1Password

6. **CVE Monitoring**
   - Subscribe to security advisories
   - Monitor npm security advisories feed
   - Set up alerts for packages you depend on
   - Test patches in staging before production

name: NFR: Accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA)
description: Ensure web applications are accessible to users with disabilities by following WCAG 2.1 AA standards.
template:
Web Accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA):

1. **Semantic HTML**
   - Use semantic elements: <header>, <nav>, <main>, <footer>, <article>
   - Use heading hierarchy: <h1>, <h2>, <h3> (don't skip levels)
   - Use <button> for buttons, <a> for links
   - Use <form>, <label>, <input> for forms
   - Anti-pattern: <div onClick={...}> without role

2. **Color Contrast**
   - Text: 4.5:1 minimum (normal text)
   - Text: 3:1 minimum (large text, 18pt+)
   - Graphical elements: 3:1 minimum
   - Don't rely on color alone to convey information
   - Tool: WebAIM contrast checker

3. **Keyboard Navigation**
   - All functionality accessible via keyboard (Tab, Enter, Space)
   - Never remove focus indicator without replacement
   - Use tabindex=0 sparingly (breaks natural tab order)
   - Test: Tab through page, no keyboard traps

4. **Assistive Technology (AT)**
   - Use aria-label, aria-labelledby for unlabeled elements
   - Use aria-live for dynamic content updates
   - Use role="button" for custom button elements
   - Test with screen readers: NVDA (Windows), JAWS (Windows), VoiceOver (Mac)

5. **Image Alt Text**
   - Provide descriptive alt text for all images
   - Decorative images: alt=""
   - Avoid: alt="image", alt="photo", alt="bullet point"
   - Example: alt="User dashboard showing sales metrics for Q4 2024"

6. **Motion & Animation**
   - Respect prefers-reduced-motion media query
   - Pattern: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { * { animation: none !important } }
   - Don't auto-play videos with sound
   - Provide captions for video content

7. **Automated Testing**
   - Lighthouse Accessibility audit (aim for 90+)
   - axe DevTools browser extension
   - ESLint plugin: eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y
   - CI/CD integration: axe-core in tests

name: NFR: Scalability & System Architecture
description: Design systems that scale horizontally and handle growing traffic and data volume.
template:
Scalability Best Practices:

1. **Horizontal Scaling**
   - Stateless servers (no in-memory caches)
   - Use distributed caches: Redis, Memcached
   - Database sharding strategy
   - Load balancer: nginx, HAProxy, cloud provider LB

2. **Database Optimization**
   - Index frequently queried columns
   - Use query result caching: Redis
   - Implement connection pooling
   - Monitor slow queries; optimize N+1 queries

3. **API Rate Limiting**
   - Implement per-user rate limits
   - Use sliding window or token bucket algorithm
   - Return 429 status with Retry-After header
   - Pattern: express-rate-limit middleware

4. **CDN & Caching Strategy**
   - Use CDN for static assets (Cloudflare, Vercel, AWS CloudFront)
   - Set Cache-Control headers: public, max-age=31536000
   - Use cache busting: hash filenames
   - Monitor cache hit rates; optimize as needed

5. **Monitoring & Observability**
   - Collect metrics: response time, error rate, throughput
   - Set up alerts: high error rate, slow response time
   - Use APM tools: DataDog, New Relic, Sentry
   - Log structured data (JSON); avoid free-form text

6. **Capacity Planning**
   - Monitor growth trends
   - Estimate resource needs 6-12 months ahead
   - Conduct load testing before peak seasons
   - Have auto-scaling policies in place
